title: Tenn Graneet
content:
{{Top|leg|canon=Unidentified Death Star gunner}} {{Character |type=Galactic Empire |image=[[File:Tenn_graneet.png]] |name=Tenn Graneet |homeworld= |birth=Prior to [[50 BBY/Legends|50 BBY]][[Death Star (novel)|''Death Star'']] |death=[[0 BBY/Legends|0 BBY]], [[DS-1 Orbital Battle Station/Legends|''Death Star'']], [[Yavin system/Legends|Yavin system]]{{Film|IV}} |species=[[Human/Legends|Human]] |gender=[[Gender/Legends|Male]] |pronouns=new |height= |mass= |hair= |feathers= |eyes= |skin= |cyber= |families=new |parents=new |partners=new |siblings=new |children=new |affiliation=*[[Galactic Republic/Legends|Galactic Republic]] **[[Republic Navy/Legends|Republic Navy]] *[[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]] **[[Imperial Navy/Legends|Imperial Navy]] |masters= |apprentices= }} {{Quote|Sir?''"''
''"''Pack your bags, Chief. You're for the Death Star. They're giving you the big gun.|Tenn Graneet and Nast Hoberd|}} '''Tenn Graneet''' was a [[Human/Legends|Human]] [[Gender/Legends|male]] [[Imperial gunner/Legends|Imperial gunner]] who was [[Master chief petty officer|master chief gunnery officer]] in the [[Imperial Navy/Legends|Imperial Navy]] during the [[Galactic Civil War/Legends|Galactic Civil War]]. He was assigned the role of pulling the firing switch on the [[DS-1 Orbital Battle Station/Legends|Death Star's]] [[Superlaser/Legends|superlaser]], and was killed during the [[Battle of Yavin/Legends|Battle of Yavin]] when the [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Rebel Alliance]] caused the Death Star to explode. ==Biography== ===Military career=== {{Quote|I've already told my exec I'm ready to sign on. Soon as they get a gun working, enough air to breathe, and enough gravity to tell which way's up, I'm there.|Tenn Graneet to Olzal Erne on his request to be transferred to the Death Star|}} Graneet was born in [[50 BBY/Legends|50 BBY]]. He joined the [[Galactic Republic/Legends|Galactic Republic's]] [[Republic Navy/Legends|Judicial Starfleet]] in [[30 BBY/Legends|30 BBY]] and Graneet served as an assistant gunner when the [[Clone Wars/Legends|Clone Wars]] began. Near the end of the war, on his first assignment, a loader switched leads on a heavy capacitor and forgot to set the safeties, showering the gun crew with shrapnel, a piece of which severed the tendon connecting Graneet’s right pectoral muscle to his arm. Graneet underwent surgery and received a physical modification that increased the strength in his right arm by thirty percent. After the war, Graneet continued to serve as the Republic as it transitioned into the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]]. At one point, his nephew, [[Hora Graneet]], joined the Imperial Navy out of admiration for his uncle, and was killed onboard the [[Imperial II-class Star Destroyer/Legends|''Imperial II''-class Star Destroyer]] ''[[Battle Lance]]''. Tenn, who had considered Hora to be his favorite nephew, felt personally responsible for his death. By [[3 BBY/Legends|3 BBY]], Graneet rose to the rank of [[Master chief petty officer]] and was stationed onboard the [[Imperial-class Star Destroyer/Legends|Star Destroyer]] ''[[Steel Talon/Legends|Steel Talon]]''. Having heard of the Empire's new under construction battlestation, the [[Death Star/Legends|Death Star]], he told his [[Executive officer/Legends|executive officer]] he wanted to be signed on to serve aboard it. ===Serving on the Death Star=== {{Quote|I'm not a politician or a Moff, sir. I do my job, let them do theirs.|Graneet to his CO shortly before the ''Fortessa''{{'s}} destruction|}} [[File:DeathStar1-SWE.png|left|thumb|220x220px|Tenn Graneet served aboard the first Death Star as its master chief gunnery officer.]] As the Death Star neared completion, in [[0 BBY/Legends|0 BBY]], Graneet was transferred to the station and put in charge of the [[Superlaser/Legends|superlaser]] gunnery crew, as he was among those Imperial gunners who were the highest-ranking and best trained. Graneet was initially thrilled with the new assignment, but as he familiarized himself with the new superweapon, he began to question whether any person was capable of wielding such power wisely. He rationalized his concerns with the mistaken belief that the Empire would not actually fire the weapon at full power on an inhabited planet, rather on uninhabited moons, planets, and other bodies. During the final months of construction, Graneet and his crew trained on a simulator of the main firing control chamber, familiarizing themselves with the firing sequence and working to shorten the firing sequence as much as possible. Graneet took pride in his abilities and his crew, and was glad to be the first to test the Death Star's superlaser. As the NCO IC of the superlaser's gunnery crew, it fell to Graneet to actually complete the firing sequence by pulling the firing lever. The first time he actually fired the weapon was against an old [[Lucrehulk-class battleship/Legends|''Lucrehulk'']]-class battleship, ''[[Fortressa]]'', that was being used by the [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Rebel Alliance]]. Even though the weapon was only at four percent power, it was more than enough to completely destroy the Rebel capital ship. Eventually he grew such a dislike of his job, he started to wonder about other possibilities, and stated that it was his dream to become a commander of a [[Super-class Star Destroyer|''Super''-class Star Destroyer]]. Graneet later fired the superlaser against the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] of [[Despayre]]. The destruction of Despayre took three shots to complete over the course of several hours. The first shot killed every living thing on the planet and cooked the surface of the planet. The second caused further tectonic stress to the planet, and the third shot finally blew the planet apart. After this, Graneet was highly shaken as he began to realize how much power the Death Star really had. He felt extremely guilty to have been the one to destroy Despayre, even though its main population had been that of convicted criminals. Later, Graneet was ordered to fire on the inhabited planet of [[Alderaan/Legends|Alderaan]]—killing nearly two billion sentients in the process. ===Battle of Yavin and death=== {{Quote|Stand by. Stand by…|Tenn Graneet's last words|}} Following the destruction of Despayre and Alderaan, Graneet had a severe pang of conscience. Knowing that he had sent billions to their deaths, he soon came to see himself as the largest mass murderer in galactic history, and knew that if somehow the [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Rebel Alliance]] were to win the war, he would be tried and executed for his crimes. As news that it was Graneet who fired on Despayre and Alderaan spread throughout the Death Star's population, many of the people who Graneet encountered began reacting differently to him. He also seriously began to doubt if he would force himself to destroy another planet when ordered to, but worried that if he refused, someone else would simply pull the lever for him. He even began to doubt his own loyalty to the Empire, and began to hate them and himself for causing the death of so many innocent civilians. [[File:Exds.png|thumb|220x220px|Graneet died when the first Death Star was destroyed by [[Luke Skywalker/Legends|Luke Skywalker]] during the [[Battle of Yavin/Legends|Battle of Yavin]].]] During the Battle of Yavin, when given the order to fire the superlaser at the rebel base on [[Yavin 4/Legends|Yavin 4]], Graneet froze, wracked with guilt and wishing desperately for something to save him from his dreadful duty. In desperation, he informed his gunnery crew and the overbridge to "stand by" and then repeated his order a few seconds later, until his wish was granted, as his few moments of hesitation were sufficient to allow [[Luke Skywalker/Legends|Luke Skywalker]] to destroy the Death Star. Graneet died in the resulting explosion. Incidentally, his hesitation also allowed the Rebels to eliminate not only a significant force of manpower and materials fielded by the Empire, but also some of the most achieved and capable of the Empire's officer corps, including Grand Moff Tarkin. ==Personality and traits== {{Quote|You gonna go for it?''"''
''"''Now you're kidding. I'm a lifer; why wouldn’t I? When this thing is finished, nothing anybody anywhere can field against it will even scratch the finish. Running a gun that will pop Star Destroyers like soap bubbles, maybe even knock a moon out of orbit—what kind of gunner would pass that up? Bigger is better.|Tenn Graneet to Olzal Erne on transferring to the Death Star|}} Graneet was a lifelong military man, who enjoyed his career and was always eager to be placed in front of the largest gun available. However, the fulfillment of his wish gave Tenn only feelings of shame and remorse over killing two planets, committed by him as a chief superlaser operator. His developing mental instability drove him to delay the order to shoot upon Yavin IV, thus allowing rebels to destroy the Imperial superweapon. ==Behind the scenes== The gunner that mans the superlaser in [[Star Wars: Episode IV A New Hope|''Star Wars'': Episode IV ''A New Hope'']] was identified in the [[Death Star (novel)|''Death Star'']] novel as Master Chief Gunnery Officer Tenn Graneet. Contrary to the nature of the character's responsibility, ''Death Star'' portrays Graneet as a morally conflicted individual who hated his actions but found himself forced to carry them out. ==Appearances== *''[[Star Wars: Battlefront]]'' *[[Death Star (novel)|''Death Star'']] {{1stID|Tenn Graneet}} *[[Star Wars: X-Wing (video game)|''Star Wars: X-Wing'']] *{{Film|IV}} {{1st}} *[[Children of the Jedi (novel)|''Children of the Jedi'']] {{Imo}} ===Non-canon appearances=== *[[Tag & Bink Are Dead 2|''Tag & Bink Are Dead'' 2]] ==Sources== *{{InsiderCite|96|Jawa's Corner}} *''[[The Complete Star Wars Encyclopedia]]'' *''[[The Essential Reader's Companion]]'' ==Notes and references== {{Reflist}} {{Interlang |de=Tenn Graneet |es=Tenn Graneet |fi=Tenn Graneet |nl=Tenn Graneet }} {{DEFAULTSORT:Graneet, Tenn}} [[Category:Death Star residents]] [[Category:Humans]] [[Category:Imperial Navy personnel of the Galactic Empire]] [[Category:Males]] [[Category:Republic Navy personnel]]
